title: pimelea curviflora subsp. micrantha
intro: Pimelea curviflora subsp. micrantha is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae, commonly known as the 'native currant bush'. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Pimelea curviflora subsp. micrantha include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from pain and fever. Pimelea curviflora subsp. micrantha can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
